name: Amsa
description: Amsa, in Hindu mythology, is one of the twelve Adityas, solar deities who are sons of Aditi and Kashyapa. Amsa is primarily associated with the distribution of wealth and inheritance. He is considered a protector of property and is invoked to ensure fair division and prevent disputes over possessions. As an Aditya, Amsa embodies a specific aspect of the sun's power, in his case, the regulation and equitable allocation of resources. He is often depicted as a benevolent deity who ensures that everyone receives their due share. Amsa's role is crucial in maintaining harmony within families and communities by presiding over matters of inheritance and wealth distribution. He is also believed to have the power to remove obstacles in the path of those who seek justice in property disputes. While not as widely worshipped as some other Adityas like Surya or Vishnu, Amsa's importance lies in his role as a divine arbitrator and guardian of fairness in financial matters. He embodies the principle of righteous distribution and serves as a reminder of the importance of honesty and integrity in dealings with property and inheritance. His influence extends to ensuring social order and preventing conflicts arising from unequal distribution of resources. Amsa's blessings are sought to ensure prosperous and just outcomes in all matters related to wealth and inheritance.
subtitle: Distributor of Wealth and Inheritance

keyMyths.title: Amsa's Role in Inheritance
keyMyths.description: Amsa ensures fair division of property among siblings, preventing disputes and fostering harmony. His blessings are sought for equitable outcomes in inheritance matters.

keyMyths.title: Amsa as a Protector of Property
keyMyths.description: Amsa safeguards property from theft and injustice, ensuring that rightful owners are protected. He is invoked to remove obstacles in property-related matters.

keyMyths.title: Amsa's Connection to the Sun
keyMyths.description: As an Aditya, Amsa embodies a specific aspect of the sun's power related to regulation and distribution. He ensures that resources are allocated fairly and justly.

sources.author: A.G. Krishna Warrier
